subject: Cloud Computing
title: AWS Launches and Changes Everything
description: A fast breakdown of AWS Launches and Changes Everything for builders.

hook.text: On March 14, 2006, Amazon launched S3 — simple online storage. The cloud era began.

buildup.text: Months later, EC2 let anyone rent virtual servers by the hour. No contracts, no hardware.

discovery.text: Startups could launch with zero upfront infrastructure cost. Just a credit card.

twist.text: AWS didn't target enterprises first. It targeted scrappy startups nobody else served.

climax.text: Those startups grew into Netflix, Airbnb, and Slack — all built on AWS.

punchline.text: AWS bet on startups. The startups bet on everything.

quiz.question: What was the first AWS service launched in 2006?
options: S3, a simple online storage service
EC2, a virtual server rental service
